hero.eyebrow: The Wedding Shoe Game, live with all your guests
hero.headline: Two shoes. One question. A room that <accent>holds its breath.</accent>
hero.lead: In the shoe game the couple answers by raising shoes. With TwoSoles the whole room votes first on their phones. The projector reveals whether the majority really knows the couple.
hero.imageAlt: Wedding couple raising shoes during the shoe game

items.quote: “I set it up two hours before my best friend's wedding. The room completely lost it at the first reveal.”
items.name: Lena M.

items.quote: “140 guests, zero tech problems. QR code on the projector, thirty seconds later everyone had voted. Even our grandma, and she got every question right.”
items.name: Daniel R.

items.quote: “Tested it at the pre-wedding party, played it again at the wedding. Both times the moment of the evening. One question reopened a ten-year debate.”

faq.title: Briefly <accent>answered</accent>
faq.note: Didn't find your answer? Contact our <support>support</support>.

items.question: What is the shoe game and how does it work?
items.answer: In the shoe game the couple sits back to back, each holding one of their own shoes and one of the other person's. For every question along the lines of “Who is more likely to …?” both answer who it fits. What makes TwoSoles special: the whole room votes on their phones first. The reveal happens in two steps, the couple first, then the audience, so you see whether the room read the two right.

items.question: Shoes or phones? What's the difference?
items.answer: You choose the game type when you set up the event. In classic shoe mode the couple raises real shoes, and you as host quickly enter what the two showed. In phone mode the couple answers secretly on two of their own phones, and the screen reveals everything automatically. The feel stays the same, only the way the couple answers differs.

items.question: How long does the game take, roughly?
items.answer: Plan for around 20 to 25 minutes for 10 to 15 questions. You set the pace yourself and can always ask more or fewer questions.

items.question: Do you need an app or have to install anything?
items.answer: No, for nothing. Everything runs right in the browser, whether it's the guests, the host controls, the screen or the couple. Guests scan the QR code with their phone camera and vote right away. No app, no login, no sign-up.

items.question: Can I add my own questions?
items.answer: Yes, as many as you like. Pick from our question catalogue, roll a random set or write your own questions from scratch, tailored to your couple.

items.question: What if the venue's Wi-Fi or internet is bad?
items.answer: Voting runs over the guests' mobile data, not the venue Wi-Fi. A couple of bars of signal is enough. The device on the screen usually gets by on mobile data too.

items.question: Can we test the game before we play it live?
items.answer: Absolutely. You can run through the whole game as often as you like in test mode and reset it afterwards. Only when you deliberately set the event live does the actual play window begin. Even better is to run through the whole thing once at the venue itself, with the real projector and Wi-Fi, so every step is dialed in on the big day.

items.question: Can I use this for another celebration too (engagement, anniversary)?
items.answer: Yes. TwoSoles fits any celebration that's about the couple: engagement, civil ceremony, pre-wedding party or anniversary. You pick the matching occasion when you create the event.

faqPage.metaDescription: Every answer about TwoSoles: how the shoe game works, technology, setup, pricing and privacy. Explained in a friendly, thorough way.
faqPage.title: Frequently asked questions
faqPage.subtitle: Everything you want to know about the digital shoe game, from the tech to the payment. Can't find your question? Then contact us <contact>here</contact>.
faqPage.contactNote: Still something open? Contact our <support>support</support> and we'll help you personally.

categories.heading: The game and how it works

items.question: What is the shoe game and how does it work?
items.answer: The shoe game is a wedding classic. The couple sits back to back, and each person holds one of their own shoes and one of the other person's. For every question along the lines of “Who is more likely to …?” both raise the shoe of the person the answer fits at the same time. TwoSoles adds the whole room to the fun: all your guests tap one of the two answers on their phones first. Once voting closes, the couple raises their shoes and the projector reveals how the majority in the room voted. That way you see, with every question, whether the audience read the couple right. This is exactly where the evening's biggest laughs come from.

items.question: How long does the game take, roughly?
items.answer: Expect around 20 to 25 minutes for 10 to 15 questions. You as the host set the pace entirely: you open each question, close the vote once enough votes are in, and then reveal. If you have more time, simply add more questions, and for a shorter round just use fewer.

items.question: Do we need a host or does it run automatically?
items.answer: One person should steer the game, that's the most fun. This person takes the host controls on their own phone and opens the questions, closes the vote and reveals. A short countdown before each reveal builds the suspense automatically, but you set the flow yourself. It can be the maid of honour, the best man or anyone who enjoys hosting the evening. A microphone is nice but not required.

items.question: Are there points or a winner, or is it just about the questions?
items.answer: The shared moment is what matters, not a high score. There's no leaderboard and no official winner. On the screen you see, with every question, how the whole room did and whether it read the couple right. At the end, a short finale sums up how often the couple agreed and how well the room knew them, as a lovely closing note, not a competition.

items.question: Is there sound or music, or is it silent?
items.answer: TwoSoles runs without its own audio. The suspense comes from what's on the screen and the reactions in the room, so the game never clashes with your sound system or your band. If you like, just play your own fitting music during the reveal.

items.question: Shoes or phones? What's the difference between the game types?
items.answer: When you set up the event you choose how the couple answers. In classic shoe mode the couple sits back to back and raises their shoes for each question, and you as host quickly enter in the controls what the two showed. In phone mode the couple answers secretly on two of their own phones, no shoes at all, and the screen reveals everything automatically. Either way the flow is the same: the couple is revealed first, then the audience result joins in, and at the end a finale sums up how often you agreed and how well the room knew you.

items.question: Does the couple need a phone each too?
items.answer: It depends on the game type. In classic shoe mode the couple answers only with the shoes and needs no phone. In phone mode each of the two uses their own phone to answer secretly, so two devices for the couple. Guests use their own phone in both cases, and the host controls stay with the person hosting.

items.question: Can we test the game before we play it live?
items.answer: Yes, and we even recommend it. You can run through the whole flow as often as you like, with real questions and all the views, and reset the game in the settings afterwards. Only when you deliberately set the event live does the actual 48-hour play window begin. So you can practise in peace without using anything up. The most valuable rehearsal is one right at the venue: connect the device to the real projector once, check the signal and scan the QR code with a phone. That way you know in advance that both the tech and the flow are ready.

items.question: Can I use this for another celebration too (engagement, anniversary)?
items.answer: Yes, gladly. TwoSoles fits any celebration with a couple at its heart, such as an engagement, a civil ceremony, a pre-wedding party or an anniversary. You pick the matching occasion when you create the event, and everything else works exactly the same.

items.question: Can I add my own questions?
items.answer: Yes, as many as you want. You can pick from our question catalogue, roll a random set of 15 questions with one click, or write your own from scratch. The personal insider questions tend to bring the loudest laughs, so feel free to mix classics with your own knowledge of the couple.

items.question: Can I set the order of the questions myself?
items.answer: Yes. In your question set you simply arrange the questions by drag and drop into the order you want. That way you can build a little dramaturgy and save the most exciting question for last.

items.question: Can questions be skipped if one feels awkward?
items.answer: Yes. In the host controls you see your entire question set and can jump to the next question or skip one at any time. If a question doesn't fit the mood, you simply move on and no one will notice.

items.question: Are the questions family-friendly, can kids and grandparents watch?
items.answer: Our question catalogue is deliberately charming and family-friendly, so everyone from the youngest to grandma can join in. And because you put your own set together, you have full control over the tone of the game. You decide how cheeky or how gentle your set turns out.

items.question: What language does the game run in?
items.answer: TwoSoles is currently available in German and English. When you create the event you choose the game language for the screen, the host controls and the guests' phones. That way international guests can join in with ease.

items.question: Do you need an app or have to install anything?
items.answer: No, nothing at all. Everything runs right in the browser, whether it's the guests, the host controls, the screen or the couple in phone mode. Guests scan the QR code on the screen with their normal phone camera and land straight on the voting page. No app, no store, no login and no registration. This is kept deliberately simple so that truly everyone in the room can take part.

items.question: Does it work in any browser (Safari, Chrome), or do I need an app?
items.answer: It runs in any modern browser, whether that's Safari on an iPhone, Chrome on Android or the browser on a laptop or tablet. No app is needed, since everything happens right in the browser. For the device on the screen we recommend a laptop or tablet at least 1024 pixels wide, used in the browser's fullscreen mode.

items.question: What if the venue's Wi-Fi or internet is bad?
items.answer: The guest voting runs over the guests' mobile data, not the venue Wi-Fi, so a couple of bars of signal is plenty. The device on the screen and the host controls need internet too, and mobile data or a simple hotspot is usually enough. A perfect venue Wi-Fi is explicitly not a requirement.

items.question: What happens if the internet drops briefly during the game?
items.answer: No need to worry. When a device reconnects after a short interruption, it catches up on the current game state automatically. The game progress is saved continuously, so a brief signal gap costs you neither votes nor progress. When in doubt, just wait a few seconds until everyone is connected again and then carry on.

items.question: How many devices can be connected at the same time?
items.answer: The number of guests voting at the same time depends on the package you booked, for example up to 10, 50, 100 or 300 guests. The screen and the host controls don't count towards that. Simply choose the package that matches your guest count.

items.question: What happens if we have more guests than we booked for?
items.answer: Once your package's capacity is reached, any additional guest who tries to join sees a friendly note that the game is currently full. When a spot frees up, they join automatically. If you expect more guests, you can upgrade your package beforehand at any time so everyone can play for sure.

items.question: How far in advance do I need to book?
items.answer: Not in advance at all. You can create your event in about five minutes, even on the wedding day itself. It's of course more relaxed to prepare everything a few days ahead, but it isn't necessary.

items.question: When can I set the event up, days ahead or only just before?
items.answer: Whenever suits you. You can create your event weeks ahead, set the names and colours, gather questions and upload photos, or take care of everything spontaneously right before the big moment. As long as you don't set the event live, you can keep refining it for as long as you like.

items.question: How long does access stay active, only on the wedding day or longer?
items.answer: Once you set the event live, you have exactly 48 hours to play the game. This generous window covers the whole wedding day and the day after, in case the schedule shifts. Before going live you can prepare and test the event for as long as you want. After the 48 hours the round is over, but your results stay saved for you.

items.question: What payment methods are available?
items.answer: Payment is handled securely through our payment provider Stripe. You can pay with the common credit and debit cards as well as conveniently with Apple Pay and Google Pay. Exactly which methods you see depends on your device and your country, and the options appear automatically in the payment window.

items.question: Is VAT included in the price?
items.answer: Yes. All prices shown include statutory VAT. So there are no hidden costs on top, and the price you see is the price you pay.

items.question: Can I upgrade if I tried the free tier first?
items.answer: Yes, any time. You can create your event in the small package for free first and set everything up in peace. If you later want to allow more guests, you simply upgrade to a larger package in the event settings. You only pay the difference to your previous package, not the full price again.

items.question: Can I use the game again for a second celebration, or is it tied to one event?
items.answer: Payment applies per event. For a second celebration you simply create a new event, with its own names, questions and guests. Small rounds of up to 10 guests are free, and for larger celebrations you book the matching package again. That way each celebration stays neatly separate.

items.question: Is there a refund if we end up not playing?
items.answer: As long as you haven't set the event live and therefore haven't used it yet, just get in touch and we'll find a fair solution. Once the event is live, it counts as an immediately provided digital service for which a refund is no longer possible. If you're unsure, it's best to try the free package first.

items.question: How secure is the payment?
items.answer: Very secure. The entire payment is handled by Stripe, one of the world's leading and certified payment providers. Your card details are sent directly and encrypted to Stripe and are never stored on our servers. We never see your full payment data at any point.

items.question: Do I get an invoice or receipt?
items.answer: Yes. After every payment you'll find an overview of your purchases in your account under “Invoices”, including a receipt to view. On top of that, our payment provider sends you a payment confirmation by email.

items.question: What happens to our answers and data after the wedding?
items.answer: Your guests' votes are recorded anonymously, so it's never stored who voted for what. Your questions and the results stay saved in your account after the game, so you can look back at them later. We only keep data for as long as necessary, and you'll find the details in our privacy policy.

items.question: Are photos or answers shared publicly anywhere?
items.answer: No. Your photos and content appear only on your own game screens during the event, that is on the projector and your guests' phones. None of it is shared publicly, published or used for advertising. The game stays your private moment.

items.question: I paid but access isn't working, what should I do?
items.answer: In the vast majority of cases access is unlocked within a few moments. Reload the page once and your package should appear. If it still doesn't, drop us a short email at hello@twosoles.live with your email address and the name of the event. We'll look into it as fast as we can and unlock access manually if needed.

items.question: What if I can't log in with the magic-link email?
items.answer: First check your spam or promotions folder, as these links sometimes end up there. Also make sure to open the link on the same device and in the same browser you want to sign in with. For security reasons the links are only valid for a limited time, so just request a new one if in doubt. Alternatively you can sign in with Google. If it still doesn't work, get in touch and we'll help you personally.

items.question: Who do I turn to if something doesn't work on the wedding day, is there fast support?
items.answer: You can reach us directly by email at hello@twosoles.live. We read every message ourselves and respond as quickly as we can, so your big moment doesn't stall. As a safeguard we also recommend testing the game once in advance and keeping both devices plugged in. That way you're well prepared.

imprint.title: Imprint
imprint.metaDescription: Provider and contact details for TwoSoles.

sections.heading: Provider
sections.body:
This website and the TwoSoles service are operated by:
Marvin Vrdoljak
Grimselstrasse 30
8048 Zürich
Switzerland

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: Email: hello@twosoles.live

sections.heading: Legal form and registration
sections.body: TwoSoles is run as a sole proprietorship by Marvin Vrdoljak. The business is not entered in the commercial register and is not registered for VAT. For that reason, no company identification number (UID) and no VAT number are shown.

sections.heading: Payment processing
sections.body: Paid bookings are handled through the payment provider Stripe. Stripe acts as the merchant of record, meaning it processes the payment as the responsible contracting party and takes care of calculating and remitting any applicable taxes. You can find the details in our privacy policy and terms.

sections.heading: Liability for content
sections.body: The content of this website is created with the greatest possible care. However, no guarantee is given for the accuracy, completeness or timeliness of the content. Use of the content is at your own risk.

sections.heading: Liability for links
sections.body: Our offer may contain links to external third-party websites over whose content we have no influence. The respective provider or operator of the pages is always responsible for that third-party content.

sections.heading: Copyright
sections.body: The content and works published on this website are subject to Swiss copyright law. Reproduction, editing, distribution and any kind of use beyond the limits of copyright require the prior written consent of the provider.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.metaDescription: How TwoSoles processes personal data: a transparent overview of what we collect, why and with which services.
privacy.intro: Protecting your personal data matters to us. Below we explain which data we process when you use TwoSoles, for what purposes and on what basis. The basis is the Swiss Data Protection Act (revDSG). For users in the EU and EEA, the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) additionally applies and grants you the rights set out below.

sections.heading: Controller
sections.body:
The controller responsible for data processing is:
Marvin Vrdoljak
Grimselstrasse 30
8048 Zürich
Switzerland
Email: hello@twosoles.live

sections.heading: What data we process
sections.body:
Account data: the email address and name you provide when registering.
Event and game data: the questions you create, uploaded photos of the couple and the anonymous votes cast by your guests. It is not stored which guest voted for what.
Contact data: the details you enter in our contact form (name, email address, optionally the wedding date and guest count, and your message).
Payment data: for paid bookings, the data required for payment, which is processed directly by our payment provider. We do not receive complete payment data.
Technical data: server log data such as IP address, date and time of access and details of the browser used, which arise for technical reasons.
Usage data: aggregated, anonymous statistics on the use of the website (see the section on audience measurement).

sections.heading: Purposes and legal bases
sections.body: We process your data to provide the service and to perform our contract with you (Art. 6(1)(b) GDPR), to ensure secure and stable operation and to respond to enquiries based on our legitimate interest (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR) and, where required, based on your consent (Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR). Under Swiss law, we base the processing on contract performance, our overriding legitimate interest and, where necessary, your consent.

sections.heading: Visibility of your event content
sections.body: The content of an event, in particular the couple's names, your questions and the uploaded photos, is shown during the game on the devices of participating guests and on the presenting screen. Access is only possible through an unguessable event link or QR code: only those who receive this link from you can see the content. The photos are held in private storage and are served only through short-lived, server-generated links; direct public access is not possible. The game pages are also excluded from indexing by search engines.

sections.heading: Cookies and local storage
sections.body: We only use technically necessary cookies and similar storage techniques, for example to store your login, your language choice and host access during a game. These are required for operation and do not require consent. We do not use tracking or advertising cookies.

sections.heading: Audience measurement
sections.body: To improve our service we use Vercel Analytics and Vercel Speed Insights. These services collect aggregated, anonymous information on the use and loading performance of the website, such as pages viewed and technical performance values. They work without cookies, do not create cross-device profiles and do not pass the data on for advertising purposes.

sections.heading: Email communication
sections.body: We only send you emails that are necessary for the service (transactional emails), such as the sign-in link, payment confirmations and replies to your enquiries. We do not send a newsletter or advertising emails; any promotional contact only takes place if you have expressly consented beforehand, and you can withdraw such consent at any time with effect for the future.

sections.heading: Services we use
sections.body:
For operation we use carefully selected service providers who process data on our behalf:
Hosting and audience measurement: Vercel Inc., USA.
Database, authentication, real-time synchronisation and photo storage: Supabase, with servers located in the European Union (Frankfurt).
Sign in with Google (optional): Google Ireland Limited. This data exchange only takes place if you choose to log in with Google.
Payment processing: Stripe. Stripe acts as the merchant of record and processes the payment data on its own responsibility, including tax handling.
Contact form: Resend, which processes the details sent through the form and delivers them to us by email.
Where required, data processing agreements are in place with these providers.

sections.heading: International data transfers
sections.body: Some of the providers named process data outside Switzerland, in particular in the European Union and the USA. Where data is transferred to a country without an equivalent level of data protection, we ensure appropriate safeguards, such as standard contractual clauses, to protect your data adequately.

sections.heading: Retention period
sections.body: We store your data only for as long as necessary for the stated purposes or as required by statutory retention periods. Account and event data are stored for as long as your account exists. If you delete your account or an event, the associated data is removed. Afterwards the data is deleted or anonymised.

sections.heading: Your rights
sections.body: You have the right to access the data processed about you, as well as to rectification, erasure and restriction of processing. You also have the right to data portability and a right to object, and you can withdraw any consent given at any time with effect for the future. To do so, simply contact the address above. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with a supervisory authority. In Switzerland this is the Federal Data Protection and Information Commissioner (FDPIC), and in the EU the data protection authority responsible for you.

sections.heading: Data security
sections.body: We take appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect your data against unauthorised access, loss or misuse. Data is transmitted in encrypted form over secure connections (HTTPS).

sections.heading: Automated decision-making
sections.body: We do not carry out automated decision-making, including profiling, within the meaning of the GDPR.

sections.heading: Minors
sections.body: Our service is intended for persons of legal age. We do not knowingly process data of children. Should we become aware that data of minors has been provided to us without the required consent, we will delete it.

sections.heading: Data protection contact
sections.body: If you have any questions about data protection or wish to exercise your rights, you can reach us at: hello@twosoles.live.

sections.heading: Changes to this privacy policy
sections.body: We may amend this privacy policy, for example in the event of changes to our service or the legal situation. The current version published on this page always applies.

terms.title: Terms & Conditions
terms.metaDescription: The terms and conditions governing the use of TwoSoles.
terms.intro: These Terms and Conditions govern the use of the TwoSoles service, offered by Marvin Vrdoljak. Please read them carefully.

sections.heading: 1. Scope and provider
sections.body: These terms apply to the use of the online service provided under TwoSoles. The provider is Marvin Vrdoljak, Grimselstrasse 30, 8048 Zürich, Switzerland (the “provider”). By registering and using the service you agree to these terms. We do not accept conflicting terms unless we expressly consent to them.

sections.heading: 2. Description of services
sections.body: TwoSoles is a digital version of the wedding shoe game. The provider offers a web application that lets guests vote live via QR code and presents the results on a screen. The specific scope of features, in particular the number of guests taking part at the same time, depends on the chosen package. The provider develops the service on an ongoing basis and may adjust individual features, provided the essential service remains intact.

sections.heading: 3. Registration and account
sections.body: A free account is required to use the service. Sign-in is passwordless via a login link sent by email or via a supported third-party login. You are obliged to provide truthful information and to keep your login credentials confidential. The service is intended for persons who are at least 18 years old and have full legal capacity. You can delete your account at any time in your account settings; the associated data is removed in the process.

sections.heading: 4. Conclusion of contract
sections.body: The presentation of the packages in the service is an invitation to submit an offer. By creating a free event or by confirming a paid booking, you submit a binding offer. The contract for the use of the service is concluded between you and the provider as soon as the event is created or the booking is confirmed.

sections.heading: 5. Prices, payment and taxes
sections.body:
Use is charged as a one-off payment per event. The price depends on the booked guest capacity; small events of up to ten guests are free. All prices include any applicable taxes.
Payment is processed by Stripe. Stripe acts as the merchant of record, meaning that Stripe handles the payment as the responsible contracting party for the payment, issues the payment confirmation and takes care of calculating and remitting any applicable taxes. Stripe's own terms may additionally apply to the payment. The data required for payment is transmitted directly to Stripe; the provider does not receive complete payment data.
An upgrade to a larger package is possible at any time; in that case only the difference to the package already paid for is charged.

sections.heading: 6. Provision and play window
sections.body: After creating it, you can prepare, edit and test your event without any time limit. Once you deliberately set the event live, the 48-hour play window begins, within which the game can be played. Each event can be set live only once. After the 48 hours the round is over; the saved results remain available in your account.

sections.heading: 7. Withdrawal and refunds
sections.body: For online contracts, Swiss law provides no statutory right of withdrawal or return. Regardless of this: as long as a paid event has not yet been set live and therefore has not been used, you can contact the provider, who will offer you a refund as a goodwill gesture. Once the event is set live, the service counts as an immediately and fully provided digital service for which a refund is excluded. We recommend testing the service in the free package beforehand.

sections.heading: 8. User obligations and content
sections.body: You are responsible for the content you create, in particular your own questions and uploaded photos, and you warrant that you hold the necessary rights and do not infringe the rights of third parties. Where individuals are recognizable in uploaded photos, you warrant that they have consented to the storage and display within the service (right to one's own image). You undertake not to misuse the service, in particular not to submit unlawful, offensive or harmful content. In the event of serious or repeated violations, misuse, unlawful content or non-payment, the provider may temporarily block access or terminate the account. In that case the account and the associated data are deleted; paid services that have already been fully provided are not refunded. You grant the provider the simple, term-limited right to store your content and display it within the service, to the extent necessary to provide it. All rights to the software, the brand and the provider's own content remain with the provider.

sections.heading: 9. Availability
sections.body: The provider strives to keep the service available with as little interruption as possible but does not owe uninterrupted availability. Maintenance, technical faults, dependencies on third parties and force majeure may lead to temporary restrictions. Taking part also requires a working internet connection on the devices involved, which is outside the provider's control. The service is not a data backup service. You should independently save results that are important to you, for example by export or screenshot, as the provider does not owe permanent retention or restoration.

sections.heading: 10. Liability
sections.body: The provider is liable without limitation for damage caused intentionally or through gross negligence. In the case of slight negligence, the provider is only liable for the breach of essential contractual obligations and limited to the foreseeable damage typical for the contract. Any further liability is excluded to the extent permitted by law. Mandatory statutory liability provisions remain unaffected.

sections.heading: 11. Data protection
sections.body: Information on the processing of personal data can be found in our privacy policy.

sections.heading: 12. Changes to these terms
sections.body: The provider may amend these terms, for example in the event of changes to the service or the legal situation. You will be informed of material changes in an appropriate manner. For bookings already concluded, the version valid at the time of booking applies.

sections.heading: 13. Governing law and place of jurisdiction
sections.body: Swiss law applies, excluding its conflict-of-law rules and the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods. The place of jurisdiction is Zürich, unless mandatory statutory places of jurisdiction apply. For consumers, the mandatory place of jurisdiction at their domicile is reserved. For consumers with their habitual residence in the European Union, the mandatory consumer protection provisions of their country of residence also remain unaffected; the choice of law does not deprive them of the protection afforded by those provisions. Should individual provisions of these terms be invalid, the validity of the remaining provisions remains unaffected.

couple.title: Who's being put to the test?
couple.subtitle: Both names appear on the projector and the voting buttons. Photos are optional.
couple.colorHint: This colour represents the person throughout the game: on the guests' voting buttons and in the result bar on the screen.

details.title: The key facts of the celebration
details.subtitle: Title, date and language. The guest count comes later. Build first, decide later.
details.themeHint: Sets the default look of the game screens (projector, guest phones and host). Light suits daytime celebrations, dark suits evening venues. The host can switch it any time during the game.
details.answerModeHint: Classic: the couple raises real shoes and the host taps in who they meant once voting closes. On the phone: the couple answers secretly on two of their own devices and the screen reveals both at once. Either way, the finale shows how often you agreed and how well the room knows you.

goLiveConfirm.title: Really set the event live?
goLiveConfirm.text: Please think this through. It can't be undone:
points: You can set each event live only ONCE.
From going live you have exactly 48 hours to play the game.
After the 48 hours the event is permanently over and can't be started again.

introOverlay.title: Your event is in preparation
introOverlay.intro: Before the big day kicks off, a quick heads-up:
points: Your event is in preparation right now, basically the test phase. Up to 10 guests can join here, so you can try the game out in peace first.
To let as many guests play as your package allows, you need to set the event live once it's really starting.
Note: the event can be set live only once. From then you have exactly 48 hours to play the game.

settings.statusDraftText: Your event is in preparation. In this phase up to 10 guests can join so you can test in peace. Once it's really starting, set the event live: only then can as many guests play as your package allows. From going live you have 48 hours, after which the event is closed and cannot be started again.

settings.statusLiveText: Your event is live. Share the guest code with your guests. They don't need an app.
settings.statusEndedText: The event has ended. The results stay saved for you.

resetGame.cardText: Clears the saved game progress, the current votes and results, so the next host session starts over in the lobby. Your questions and settings stay untouched.
resetGame.title: Reset the game?
resetGame.text: All saved votes and results are lost and the game starts over in the lobby. This cannot be undone.
resetGame.error: The game could not be reset. Please try again.

guide.title: How to play TwoSoles
guide.intro: The digital shoe game, live with the whole room. Plan around 20 to 25 minutes for 10 to 15 questions. This guide explains both ways to play, the classic shoe mode and phone mode, so you're ready either way. You can see which mode you chose in the key facts. Read along or <pdf>download as a PDF</pdf>.
guide.idea: Every question is a “Who's more likely to?”. There are always exactly two answers to choose from: person 1 or person 2. The whole room votes on their phones, and the couple answers too. There are two ways to play, which you pick when you create the event: in the classic shoe mode the couple raises shoes, in phone mode they answer secretly on two of their own phones. At the end of each question you compare whether the room read the couple right. A short finale sums up how often the couple agreed and how well the room knows them.
needs: A large screen so everyone in the room can see the projector view. That can be a projector with a screen, a big TV or a monitor.
A device you connect to the screen. We recommend a laptop or tablet at least 1024px wide.
A second device for the host controls, which you use to run the game. Laptop, tablet or phone.
An internet connection for the device on the screen and the host device. Guests need internet too. Mobile data is usually enough.
For the classic shoe mode only: two chairs and a pair of shoes from each of the two. In the end each person holds one of their own shoes and one of the other person's.
For phone mode only: two of the couple's own phones, one per person, with the couple link open.
guide.viewsIntro: Before you start, it's worth a look at the four views the game runs across. You only need the couple view in phone mode.

views.title: Projector view
views.text: Runs on the screen and is visible to everyone. It shows the welcome screen and the current question.

views.title: Host controls
views.text: Your device, which you use to run the game. Here you open questions, close the vote, reveal, and find your question set and a few settings.

views.title: Guest view
views.text: The game view for guests, which opens on their phone via QR code. It shows the current question and the two answers to choose from.

views.title: Couple view (phone mode only)
views.text: The secret link for the couple. Open it on both of the couple's phones, pick who is who, and tap your answer to every question. In shoe mode this view stays unused.

setup: Connect the first device to the screen and open the <display>projector view</display> on it. It's best to use your browser's fullscreen mode. On a second device, ideally a smartphone, you open the <host>host controls</host>.

Finally, make sure the game is reset so it starts from the beginning. You do this in this event's <settings>settings</settings> or in the <host>host panel</host>. After that the screen sits on the welcome screen and you're ready to go.

Now prepare the couple, depending on how you play. In shoe mode, set up two chairs back to back, ideally in front of or next to the screen, so the couple sits back to back and can't see each other. Each person is handed one of their own shoes and one of the other person's. In phone mode, hand the couple the two couple links, one per person, and both open their link on their own phone and pick which partner they are. Either way, make sure the couple and the screen are clearly visible to everyone in the room.

It's best to set the event live in the <settings>settings</settings> ahead of time, not at the last moment. Only then can as many guests play as your package allows. In preparation it's a maximum of 10, so you can test in peace beforehand. Note: you can go live only once, and from then you have exactly 48 hours, so plan it so your event falls within that 48-hour window.

round.title: Guests connect.
round.text: A QR code appears on the screen for everyone to see. Guests scan it with their phone camera and land right on the voting page, with no app and no login. Tip: you can also place the QR codes on the tables. Grab the printable PDF <download>here</download>.

round.title: Ask a question.
round.text: You open the first question in the <host>host controls</host>. It appears large on the screen, and guests tap person 1 or person 2 on their phone. In phone mode the couple taps its answer on their own phones at the same time. In the host controls you see live how many votes are already in.

round.title: Close the vote.
round.text: Once enough votes are in, you close the vote. The screen now shows “Closed. The reveal is coming.”, but the result stays hidden. In shoe mode this is exactly when the couple raises their shoes: each person raises the shoe of whoever they're voting for (their own shoe means “me”, the other person's means “you”), and you quickly enter in the controls what the two showed. In phone mode the couple has already answered on their own phones alongside the audience, so there's nothing to enter. Both decide independently and are welcome to disagree.

round.title: Reveal.
round.text: You press “Reveal”. After a short countdown, the result appears. In phone mode it happens in two steps: first the screen shows only the couple, what each person said and whether the two agree, then with one more click you bring in the audience result and the screen tells you directly whether the room read the couple right. In shoe mode the screen shows the audience result directly, which you compare with the couple's raised shoes.

round.title: Keep going.
round.text: With “Next question” you work through your set. After the last question, the finale shows how often the couple agreed and how well the room knew them.

tips: 10 to 15 questions is ideal. Enough tension without dragging.
Insider questions bring the loudest laughs. Mix classics with your personal couple knowledge.
Keep all devices plugged in, including the couple's two phones in phone mode, so nothing dies mid-game.
Wait to close until enough guests have voted. You see the vote count live in the controls.
Do a short dry run beforehand, ideally right at the venue with the real projector and Wi-Fi, so every step is dialed in on the big day.
guide.downloadHint: Got everything? Print the guide for the big day.

home.title: TwoSoles
home.metaTitle: TwoSoles · The digital wedding shoe game
home.metaDescription: The wedding shoe game live with every guest: the whole room votes on their phone and the projector reveals the result. No app, no login. Ready in five minutes.
